text:
There once was a fisherman who'd lost everything. He'd lost his nets, his boats, his friends... even his love.

One foggy morning, he found an old net in a snag. That's all. Just one old net. He figured he could use it.

Tomorrow morning. He'd anchor offshore. Catch some shrimp.

Mid-night. He was rowing back.

A crab.

It clamped its claw onto his line.

Crash. Something big.

He pulled out a turtle.

It hadn't moved.

He couldn't.

He kept pulling.

I'm sorry.

Now just keep.

He found himself drifting.

That's okay.

Just let me go.

He let her go.

That night.

He slept.

They come every spring. It's my favorite season.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>
